expected_behavior: SubmitAction maps a one-based action id from the current state to Session.ApplyAction and increments version only after accepted actions

expected_behavior: Advance polls the active controller seat using CLI/runner phase order until the human has legal actions or the match completes

expected_behavior: Concede completes the current match for the human seat; NextMatch records the result and starts the next consecutive match

error: Initial make check failed on gocritic hugeParam for Bubble Tea value receivers

user_story: As a local player I can continue or exit after a match
expected_behavior: q exits, c concedes, n starts the next match from complete state

user_story: As a product/engineering owner I know whether to freeze protobuf now
expected_behavior: ADR compares proven client DTOs against CLI, TUI, daemon, and future non-Go frontend needs, and records the decision to defer protobuf until a real process/frontend boundary appears

user_story: As a future daemon layer I can manage local tables without transport code
expected_behavior: Registry creates an in-memory table around client.LocalGame, joins the playable seat, serializes table mutation with a per-table mutex, submits actions with a version guard, and rejects stale state versions
error: Initial registry state exposed no human legal actions until CreateTable advanced controller turns; lint also required exported error comments and no shadowed err
fix_status: fixed by advancing before publish and lint cleanup

suggested_test: go test ./cmd/durakd ./internal/app/server; go run ./cmd/durakd status; make check
error: Initial make check required handling fmt.Fprintf write error
fix_status: fixed by returning the write error

user_story: As a product/engineering owner I know which remote transport comes first
expected_behavior: Stack and architecture docs choose Wish-hosted SSH TUI before HTTP, gRPC, or protobuf APIs, and defer API transport until a real non-terminal client exists

user_story: As a terminal player I can connect to a hosted TUI over SSH
expected_behavior: durakd ssh starts a Wish server on localhost:23234, creates one local TUI game per SSH session, and stores the dev host key under user config unless overridden

user_story: As a developer I can smoke hosted TUI games with reproducible local bots
expected_behavior: durakd ssh accepts -seed for deterministic deals and -bot for simple, random, or heuristic local controllers while leaving AI provider and multiplayer table config out of scope
suggested_test: go test ./cmd/durakd ./internal/adapters/ssh; make check; SSH smoke with -seed 42 -bot random

user_story: As a hosted terminal player I can reconnect to one daemon-owned table while the process is alive
expected_behavior: durakd ssh accepts -table to route sessions through an in-memory Registry table; the TUI drives the same minimal game contract for local and registry-backed games
suggested_test: go test ./cmd/durakd ./internal/adapters/ssh ./internal/adapters/tui ./internal/app/server; make check; SSH smoke with -table demo

user_story: As two hosted terminal players we can play different seats at one SSH table
expected_behavior: durakd ssh -table creates a two-human in-memory table; SSH exec commands seat 0 and seat 1 attach sessions to separate hands, reserve occupied seats, release them on close, and refresh TUI state periodically
suggested_test: go test ./cmd/durakd ./internal/adapters/ssh ./internal/adapters/tui ./internal/app/server; make check; SSH smoke with -table demo seat 0/1

user_story: As a hosted table owner I can choose table size and which seats are human
expected_behavior: durakd ssh -table accepts -seats and -human-seats; non-human table seats use the selected local bot controller, and the TUI shows a waiting message when another seat must act
suggested_test: go test ./cmd/durakd ./internal/adapters/ssh ./internal/adapters/tui ./internal/app/server; make check; sentrux check_rules

user_story: As a player I start a local CLI game
expected_behavior: No subcommand runs play mode, prints the game header/help, starts Match #1, renders state, and prompts for input

user_story: As a player I want reproducible deals and supported rules
expected_behavior: -seed creates deterministic deals; -rules currently accepts default and rejects unknown presets

user_story: As a player I choose player count and my seat
expected_behavior: -seats configures 2..6 seats; -human-seat must be inside 0..seats-1; exactly one local human is supported

user_story: As a player I configure controllers for non-human seats
expected_behavior: -bot sets the default controller and -p0..-p5 override seats; supported controllers are simple, random, heuristic, ai-raw-mock, ai-raw-exec, ai-openai

user_story: As a player I see the current position and legal moves
expected_behavior: CLI renders phase, seats, trump, stock, discard, hand sizes, table, hand, and numbered legal actions

user_story: As a player I choose a listed move by number
expected_behavior: Input 1..len(legal actions) selects the corresponding legal action; invalid/out-of-range numbers do not mutate the match

user_story: As the attacker I attack with a card
expected_behavior: a/attack with a hand index or card code selects a legal attack action

user_story: As the defender I defend a specific attack
expected_behavior: d/defend selects a legal defense card, optionally filtered by one-based attack number

user_story: As an eligible seat I throw in, pass, or transfer
expected_behavior: throw/th/add selects throw-in, pass declines or falls back to done, and tr/transfer selects legal transfer

user_story: As a defender or attacker I take cards or finish a stage
expected_behavior: take/t selects take; done/finish/f selects FinishDefense or FinishTake when legal

user_story: As a player I concede the current match
expected_behavior: concede/surrender/ff completes the match with the acting seat as loser and renders the result

user_story: As a player I ask for help or exit
expected_behavior: help/h/? prints help; quit/q/exit prints Bye and exits the loop

user_story: As a player I start another match after a result
expected_behavior: After completion, Enter or next starts the next match, quit exits, help prints next-match help, and invalid input is rejected

user_story: As a human I wait while bots take their turns
expected_behavior: When the human has no legal actions, CLI chooses the active controller seat by phase, applies controller decisions through validation, and prints controller actions

user_story: As a developer I run bot matches without UI
expected_behavior: arena validates matches, seed, max actions, seats, rules, and controllers, runs SeriesRunner, and prints summary/results

user_story: As a developer I export public headless events
expected_behavior: arena -event-log appends public JSONL events with base, base-2 match ids and private file permissions

user_story: As a developer I persist arena matches to SQLite
expected_behavior: arena -db migrates SQLite and records config, internal events, public events, metadata, and completed summaries transactionally

user_story: As a developer I print move-quality aggregates for arena runs
expected_behavior: arena -eval wraps decisions, ranks accepted actions, and prints aggregate quality/loss counters

user_story: As a user I list matches from a public JSONL log
expected_behavior: history -event-log groups events by match, builds summaries, prints rows, handles empty/missing logs, and rejects mixed sources

user_story: As a user I list completed matches from SQLite
expected_behavior: history -db opens/migrates DB, reads match summaries ordered by projection time, and prints rows or no matches

user_story: As a developer I verify a stored match reconstructs correctly
expected_behavior: replay -db -match-id replays internal events with config snapshot, verifies emitted events, and prints final replay summary

user_story: As a developer I inspect visible decision quality after a match
expected_behavior: analyze -db -match-id replays internal events, builds decision contexts before actions, prints aggregate analysis and worst moves respecting limit

user_story: As a player I start with default Durak house rules
expected_behavior: Default rules use a 36-card deck, 2..6 players, six-card hands, no ace trump indicator, transfers enabled, lead-first throw-in, and all-eligible pass close

user_story: As a config user I get explicit rejection for unsupported future variants
expected_behavior: Validation accepts only implemented presets/options and rejects unsupported timing, cheating, deck, direction, and rule variants

user_story: As a player I receive a valid initial deal and first attacker
expected_behavior: Deal is round-robin, stock remains after hands, trump is selected from stock bottom or last dealt card, invalid setup redeals, and first attacker is lowest trump or seeded random

user_story: As a player or bot I see only public state plus my own hand
expected_behavior: SeatView exposes public state; DecisionContext adds current hand, legal actions, and public memory; returned slices are copies

user_story: As a controller I can choose only a legal action
expected_behavior: Legal actions use the same validation path as ApplyAction and session/runner reject mutated or unavailable actions

user_story: As attacker and defender I play a basic battle
expected_behavior: Only attacker attacks, only defender defends, defense must beat the attack, and the phase opens throw-in after all attacks are covered

user_story: As defender I transfer when rules allow it
expected_behavior: Transfer is legal only in defense with same rank on table, before defense, and moves defense to the next active seat

user_story: As eligible attackers I throw in and close the window
expected_behavior: Throw-in honors eligible seats, table ranks, lead-first opening, pass tracking, and attack limits; finish is available only when the window can close

user_story: As players we resolve a defended or taken round
expected_behavior: FinishDefense discards table cards, FinishTake gives them to defender, refill order is applied, roles advance, and match completion/draw is detected

user_story: As a player/controller I can concede
expected_behavior: Concede completes the match, sets loser/winner, emits concede and match-ended events, and respects TurnContext CanConcede

user_story: As a headless user I run consecutive bot matches
expected_behavior: Series uses canonical seats, previous loser affects next first attacker, runner requires controllers, polls active seats, and enforces max actions

user_story: As a user I choose simple, random, or heuristic controllers
expected_behavior: Simple is deterministic, random chooses indexed legal actions, heuristic ranks legal actions while remaining inside the controller contract

user_story: As an AI player I receive a turn prompt and return a terminal command
expected_behavior: Raw controller builds visible prompt with private hand/legal commands, rejects non-player commands, retries invalid commands, and can trace attempts

user_story: As an AI adapter user I connect subprocess or OpenAI-compatible providers
expected_behavior: Subprocess reads JSON prompt/stdout command; OpenAI-compatible calls chat completions with configured model/base/key and records private JSONL traces
